addition-wealth.note: This is a pretty boring, done to death category. There's nothing innovative here. It just does basic things using AI. We are looking for novel.

imtc.note: I'm not convinced this is a good fit. Again it looks more like a workflow automation tool to me.

avantos.note: I think we can pretty much get rid of anything that bills itself as an operating system. I am not interested in workflow for back office on anything.

wealth-com.note: way too late. I'm seeking new and novelty. This is a major incumbent, not a startup.

marloo.note: I am never looking for workflow management tools. I am not interested in supporting operations. This needs to be clear in the thesis.

asseta-ai.note: This is not a category reinvention. This is simply a Saas tool for family offices. This is not novel. You need to prioritise genuine novelty.

willow-wealth.note: Too late - have raised way too much money to be novel.

ralio.note:
This works as long as it also works for external agents
- Booked demo

openbox-ai.note:
Could be interesting if they would do the same for third party agents rather than internal workflows. 
- Booked demo

unique-ai.note:
Could be interesting. Maybe a little late for a chat.
- Booked demo

alomana.note:
Correct domain: https://alomana.com/
Worth reaching out to, although I feel like the tax harvesting use case is generated by AI and just one of a hundred.

- reached out on their website
- connected to cofounder https://www.linkedin.com/in/giuettorre/

solvapay.note: Reached out on Linkedin. Couldn't contact them via their website! I was identified as a bot.

resistant-ai.note: Novel and love the agentic security angle.

nava.note: Contacted via LinkedIn. One founder is in Portugal.

agentsmyth.note: This is what we are looking for. Shame we didn't find them sooner.

klaimee.note: Not quite as good as Mount

kimpton-ai.note: Definitely worth a look. Again, better if it exposed an agent.

serafis.note: Looks really interesting for us. Can we also find things like this in the EU? Also, this would be better if it exposed an agent.

vouched.note: This is good. May not follow up because they are in the US

t54-labs.note: This is good - we need a verification layer for agents.

sphinx.note: Probably good but not exactly ground breaking.

complyance.note: Would need to talk to them to understand whether they could help us with our use case.

certivo.note: Had this been for Finserv I would have been interested

armorcode.note: This is for security. Not interested - internal support, not breaking innovation

april.note: Series B too late. Unless their are interested in providing this as an agent. Perhaps they are working on it.

armadin.note: Raised too much. This is for procurement, not for innovation.

obin-ai.note: Internal pipelines. Again, not what I'm looking for.

opencfo.note: Not looking for in-house workflows or to make our CFO's life easier.

juno.note: I'm not looking for tax compliance so much as tax efficiencies. Agents that can help investors with clever tax strategies.

harvey.note: Workflow for a law firm. Not what we're looking for.

imper-ai.note: This is good for fraud but probably not the agentic workflow we imagine

basis.note: raised too much and for accounting firms. This is not who we serve and it is not useful to who we serve.

appzen.note: I'm not interested in internal workflow tools.

hybridity.note: Maybe they come to the US

vivox-ai.note: Not really interested in AML/KYC right now

sycamore.note: Looks like more internal workflow stuff.

novee.note: Not trying to solve problems for IT professionals.

kaaj.note: Not something I care about. Not interested in small business workflow.

linx-security.note: Just too late at series B

numos.note: I'm not trying to improve life for the finance dept.

pace.note: More back office - not interested. Also, this is a specialist back office tool which makes it even less interesting.

bank-of-bots.note: Love this concept. Looks like something I'd like to play with.

meridian-ai.note: Again, this is improvements to internal workflows.

lema-ai.note: wrong kind of risk. I'm looking for solutions that mitigate risk of third party agents.

galdera.note: Not interested in internal process improvements.

octonomy.note: Again, not interested in internal workflows right now

nexus.note: I'm not looking for internal agentic tools right now.

kai.note: This is good but a little early - there's not a lot of agentic infrastructure out there to fix security for yet.

taxdown.note: Not really interested in EU tax products or tax filing. Looking for intelligent tax systems that uncover benefits as you update your portfolio.

bretton-ai.note: Not so interested in KYC and AML.

stacks.note: Not really looking for accounting services. This is too small.
